RadioButton.android:text: Constitutional Monarchy (Monarch is ceremonial head of state)
Parliamentary Republic (President is ceremonial; Prime Minister governs)
Presidential Republic (President is both head of state and head of government)
Semi-Presidential Republic (President and Prime Minister share executive power)
A single political party representing the vanguard of the people
Religious authority as interpreted by clerical institutions

CheckBox.android:text: The state is secular (no state religion; freedom of conscience)
The state has an official religion but guarantees minority rights
The state is social (committed to welfare and reducing inequality)
The constitution is the supreme law of the land
Prohibition of torture and inhumane treatment
Prohibition of slavery and forced labor
Right to a fair and speedy trial

TextView.android:text: Question 2.3: Who has authority to suspend freedoms?

RadioButton.android:text: Executive branch with later legislative ratification
No suspension permitted under any circumstances

CheckBox.android:text: Abide by the constitution and law
Defend the country when called upon
Promote harmony and spirit of common brotherhood

RadioButton.android:text: Full judicial review (all laws and acts)

CheckBox.android:text: Active military cannot hold civil office
Military must be apolitical and loyal to constitution
War-making power rests solely with legislature
No use of armed forces for political purposes
Free movement of goods and services
Right to conserve culture and language
Protection from all forms of discrimination
